'''Hefin O'Hare''' (born 2 June 1979) is a Welsh former professional [[rugby union]] and [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played representative level rugby union (RU) for [[Scotland national rugby sevens team|Scotland 7s]], and at club level for [[New Brighton F.C. (rugby union)|New Brighton F.C.]], [[Yorkshire Carnegie|Leeds Carnegie]], [[Glasgow Warriors]], and the Scottish amateur sides [[Edinburgh Academical Football Club]], [[Dundee HSFP]] and [[Glasgow Hutchesons Aloysians RFC]], as a [[Rugby union positions#Wing|wing]], or [[Rugby union positions#Centre|centre]], and representative level rugby league (RL) for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level for [[Leeds Rhinos]], and [[Huddersfield Giants]], as a {{rlp|fb|wg|ce|hk}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Hefin O'Hare was born in [[Wrexham]], Wales,

As of 2018, he is a Chartered Building Surveyor in [[Edinburgh]],<ref name="linkedin.com"/> and regularly does charity work.<ref name="heraldscotland.com"/>

==Rugby league career==
===Professional career===
Switching code from [[rugby union]], O'Hare moved from [[Yorkshire Carnegie|Leeds Carnegie]] to [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="linkedin.com">https://uk.linkedin.com/in/hefin-o-hare-127a3a34 {{Self-published source|date=June 2022}}</ref> He played for the Leeds [[rugby league]] side from 1999 to 2000, he was then transferred to [[Huddersfield Giants]], where he played from 2000 to 2005.<ref name="linkedin.com"/>

===International career===

He played for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in the 2000 Rugby League World Cup.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.irb.com/irbsevens/edition=5/news/newsid=2030232.html#scotland+call+wales+league+star+ohare |title=International Rugby Board - NEWS |access-date=16 March 2009 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090319010553/http://www.irb.com/irbsevens/edition=5/news/newsid=2030232.html#scotland+call+wales+league+star+ohare |archive-date=19 March 2009 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

==Rugby union career==

===Amateur career===

While at the provincial district [[Glasgow Warriors]], he was to play for various Scottish amateur sides.

In the 2008–09 season, O'Hare was at [[Edinburgh Academical Football Club|Edinburgh Academicals]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.glasgowwarriors.com/articles/news/005238.php|title=Glasgow Warriors - Pro-player draft update|website=glasgowwarriors.com}}</ref>

In the 2009-10<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/scottish/8201168.stm|title=Rugby Union - Scottish - Scottish rugby draft is revealed|website=BBC Sport|date=14 August 2009}}</ref> and 2010–11 season he played for [[Dundee HSFP]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.glasgowwarriors.org/news/10/08/16/clubs-decide-201011-pro-player-draft|title=Clubs Decide 2010/11 Pro Player Draft - Glasgow Warriors|website=glasgowwarriors.org}}</ref>

After finishing his professional career, he then played for [[Glasgow Hutchesons Aloysians RFC|Glasgow Hutchesons Aloysians]].<ref name="heraldscotland.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldscotland.com/sport/13162500.Fears_of_the_long_distance_winger|title=Fears of the long-distance winger|website=The Herald|date=27 May 2014 |location=Glasgow}}</ref>

===Professional career===

O'Hare started playing rugby with [[Yorkshire Carnegie|Leeds Carnegie]]. He was with the Yorkshire club from 1997 to 1999. He then switched codes to play [[rugby league]].<ref name="linkedin.com"/>

O'Hare joined [[Glasgow Warriors]] in 2005, switching code back from [[rugby league]], to play [[rugby union]] once again.<ref name="linkedin.com"/><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.scottishrugby.org/news/05/09/02/speedy-ohare-joins-warriors|title=Speedy O'Hare Joins Warriors - Scottish Rugby Union|website=scottishrugby.org}}</ref>

===International career===

O'Hare qualified to play [[rugby union]] for Scotland after fulfilling his residency period. He stated: "It was a dream of mine to play for Scotland ever since I became eligible."<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.glasgowwarriors.com/articles/player/002750.php|title=Hefin O'Hare}}</ref>

O'Hare was capped by [[Scotland national rugby sevens team|Scotland 7s]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyunion/international/scotland/5287181/Scotland-Sevens-player-Hefin-OHare-races-to-new-Glasgow-deal.html|title=Scotland Sevens player Hefin O'Hare races to new Glasgow deal|first=Charles|last=Carrick|date=6 May 2009 }}</ref>
